message: fix(core): classify recursive CTE arms by self-reference

 {"id":"bd-3nuz","title":"§22 Verification Gates (Universal + Phase-Specific 2-9)","description":"STATUS: CLOSED (superseded rollup)

This bead is a consolidated/rolled-up version of §22 created during an earlier spec-to-beads conversion pass.

DO NOT implement from this rollup directly.

Use these replacement beads instead (plan-of-record):
- bd-331.1 — §22 Universal Verification Gates (All Phases)
- bd-331.2 — §22 Phase 1-3 Verification Gates (Foundation)
- bd-331.3 — §22 Phase 4-6 Verification Gates (Core Engine)
- bd-331.4 — §22 Phase 7-9 Verification Gates (Integration & Release)

---

## §22 Verification Gates — Complete Reference (Universal + Phase-Specific 2-9)

Every phase MUST pass ALL applicable gates before proceeding to the next phase. Gates are enforced by a gate-runner harness that blocks phase advancement on any failure.

### Universal Gates (All Phases)

These 5 gates apply to EVERY phase transition:

1. **cargo check** — `cargo check --workspace` produces zero errors AND zero warnings.
   - Verify: run command, check exit code == 0 and stderr contains no warning lines.
   - Pass: exit code 0, no warnings. Fail: any error or warning.

2. **Clippy (pedantic + nursery)** — `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` produces zero warnings with pedantic and nursery lint groups enabled.
   - Verify: run command, check exit code == 0.
   - Pass: exit code 0. Fail: any clippy warning or error.

3. **Rustfmt** — `cargo fmt --all -- --check` confirms all code is formatted.
   - Verify: run command, check exit code == 0 (no diff output).
   - Pass: exit code 0. Fail: any formatting diff detected.

4. **All tests pass** — `cargo test --workspace` passes all tests. No ignored tests without a documented reason (each #[ignore] must have a comment explaining why).
   - Verify: run command, check exit code == 0 and grep for ignored tests without doc reason.
   - Pass: all tests pass, all ignores documented. Fail: any test failure or undocumented ignore.

5. **Documentation** — `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` succeeds with all public items documented and no broken doc links.
   - Verify: run command, check exit code == 0 and no missing-docs or broken-link warnings.
   - Pass: clean doc build. Fail: any doc warning.

### Phase-Specific Gates

**Phase 2 gates (3 gates):**
- MemoryVfs passes ALL VFS trait contract tests
- Record format round-trip proptest with 10,000 iterations, zero failures
- Zero `unsafe` blocks in any crate (enforced by `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`)

**Phase 3 gates (4 gates):**
- B-tree proptest: 10,000-operation random sequence, all invariants hold
- B-tree: cursor iteration after random ops matches BTreeMap reference implementation
- Parser: 95% coverage of `parse.y` grammar productions
- Parser fuzz: 1 hour of fuzzing with zero panics

**Phase 4 gates (3 gates):**
- End-to-end: 20 SQL conformance tests (basic DDL + DML) pass
- VDBE: EXPLAIN output for basic queries matches expected opcode sequence
- Sorter: correctly sorts 100,000 rows

**Phase 5 gates (4 gates):**
- File format: database created by FrankenSQLite readable by C sqlite3
- File format: database created by C sqlite3 readable by FrankenSQLite
- WAL recovery: 100 crash-recovery scenarios with zero data loss
- RaptorQ WAL: recovery succeeds with up to R corrupted frames (R = repair symbol count)

**Phase 6 gates (13 gates):**
- MVCC stress test: 100 concurrent writers, 100 operations each, all committed rows present, no phantom rows
- SSI: write skew patterns produce abort under default serializable mode; same patterns succeed under PRAGMA fsqlite.serializable=OFF
- SSI: no false negatives (no write skew anomaly escapes detection in 3-transaction Mazurkiewicz trace exploration)
- SSI witness plane: multi-process lease expiry + TxnSlot reuse does not cause stale hot-index bits to bind to new TxnToken (TxnEpoch validation holds)
- SSI witness plane: witness objects/segments decode under injected symbol loss/reordering (repair-path succeeds or emits explicit DecodeProof)
- Snapshot isolation: verified via Mazurkiewicz trace exploration for 3-transaction scenarios (all non-equivalent orderings)
- E-process monitors: INV-1 through INV-7, zero violations over 1M operations
- GC memory bound: memory usage under sustained load stays within 2x of minimum theoretical (active transactions * pages per transaction * page size)
- Serialized mode: behavior identical to C SQLite for single-writer test suite
- Rebase merge: 1,000 merge attempts with distinct-key inserts on same page, zero false rejections
- Structured merge safety: 1,000 merge attempts with commuting, cell-key-disjoint operations on the same page, no lost updates; negative tests for B-tree lost-update counterexample (cell move/defrag vs update at old offset) never accepted
- Crash model: 100 crash-recovery scenarios validating self-healing durability contract (Section 7.9)

**Phase 7 gates (3 gates):**
- Query planner: EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN shows index usage for indexed queries
- Window functions: 50 conformance tests matching C SQLite output
- CTE: recursive CTE terminates correctly with LIMIT

**Phase 8 gates (3 gates):**
- JSON1: json_valid/json_extract/json_set pass 200 conformance tests
- FTS5: full-text search returns relevant results for 100 test queries
- R*-Tree: spatial query returns correct results for 50 bounding box queries

**Phase 9 gates (4 gates):**
- Conformance: 100% parity target across 1,000+ golden files (with any intentional divergences explicitly documented and annotated in the harness)
- Benchmarks: single-writer within 3x of C SQLite
- Benchmarks: no regression (candidate statistic <= conformal upper bound U_alpha with alpha=0.01, per §17.8 methodology) compared to Phase 8
- Replication: database replicates correctly under 10% packet loss within 1.2x of no-loss time (matches §16 Phase 9 acceptance criteria)

### Gate Totals
- Universal: 5 gates x 8 phase transitions = 40 gate checks
- Phase-specific: 3 + 4 + 3 + 4 + 13 + 3 + 3 + 4 = 37 phase-specific gates
- Grand total: 77 individual gate evaluations across all phases

### Unit Tests
- test_universal_gate_cargo_check: verify cargo check produces zero errors/warnings
- test_universal_gate_clippy: verify clippy with pedantic+nursery produces zero warnings
- test_universal_gate_fmt: verify cargo fmt --check passes
- test_universal_gate_tests: verify all tests pass, no undocumented ignores
- test_universal_gate_docs: verify cargo doc produces clean output
- test_gate_runner_reports_phase: gate runner correctly identifies current phase and applicable gates
- test_gate_runner_blocks_phase_advance: if any gate fails, phase advance is blocked
- test_gate_runner_aggregates_results: all gate results collected into structured report

### E2E Test
- Run full gate suite for current phase via gate-runner harness
- Generate JSON report: per-gate pass/fail status, duration, output summary
- Aggregate: total gates, passed, failed, skipped
- Phase readiness: boolean (all gates pass = ready to advance)
- Detailed failure log for each failed gate with stderr/stdout capture","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:17:01.894117512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:39:20.879761482Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T07:55:05.430016930Z","close_reason":"Superseded by bd-331.1 (Universal Gates), bd-331.2 (Phase 1-3), bd-331.3 (Phase 4-6), bd-331.4 (Phase 7-9). Subtasks have more detailed pass/fail criteria per gate. ### Universal Gates (All Phases)
1. `cargo check --workspace` — zero errors, zero warnings.
2. `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` — zero warnings (pedantic + nursery).
3. `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — all code formatted.
4. `cargo test --workspace` — all tests pass, no ignored tests without documented reason.
5. `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` — all public items documented, no broken doc links.

### Phase-Specific Gates
**Phase 2:** MemoryVfs passes VFS trait contract. Record format proptest 10K iterations. Zero unsafe blocks.

**Phase 3:** B-tree proptest 10K ops. Cursor = BTreeMap reference. Parser 95% parse.y coverage. Parser fuzz 1hr zero panics.

**Phase 4:** End-to-end 20 SQL conformance tests. EXPLAIN opcode sequence. Sorter 100K rows.

**Phase 5:** FrankenSQLite ↔ C sqlite3 round-trip. WAL crash recovery 100 scenarios zero loss. RaptorQ WAL recovery with R corrupted frames.

**Phase 6:** MVCC stress 100 writers × 100 ops, all rows present. SSI write skew aborts (default), succeeds (PRAGMA off). SSI no false negatives (Mazurkiewicz 3-txn). Witness plane: multi-process lease expiry + slot reuse (TxnEpoch). Witness objects decode under symbol loss. Snapshot isolation Mazurkiewicz all orderings. E-process INV-1..7 zero over 1M ops. GC memory ≤ 2x minimum theoretical. Serialized mode = C SQLite. Rebase merge 1K distinct-key zero false rejections. Structured merge safety: commuting ops no lost updates + B-tree lost-update counterexample never accepted. Crash model 100 scenarios.

**Phase 7:** Index usage in EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN. Window functions 50 conformance tests. Recursive CTE terminates with LIMIT.

**Phase 8:** JSON1 200 tests. FTS5 100 queries. R*-Tree 50 bbox queries.

**Phase 9:** **100% conformance parity** across 1K+ golden files (intentional divergences documented). Single-writer within 3x C SQLite. No regression (conformal U_alpha, alpha=0.01). Replication under 10% loss within 1.2x no-loss.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:17:02Z"},{"id":66,"issue_id":"bd-3nuz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"### Unit Tests Required for §22 Verification Gates

1. test_universal_gate_cargo_check: `cargo check --workspace` produces zero errors/warnings
2. test_universal_gate_clippy: `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` zero warnings
3. test_universal_gate_fmt: `cargo fmt --all -- --check` passes
4. test_universal_gate_tests: `cargo test --workspace` all pass, no ignored without documented reason
5. test_universal_gate_docs: `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` all public items documented
6. test_gate_runner_reports_phase: Gate runner correctly identifies current phase and applicable gates
7. test_gate_runner_blocks_phase_advance: If any gate fails, phase advance is blocked
8. test_gate_runner_aggregates_results: All gate results collected into structured report

### E2E Test (Gate Verification Harness)
Run full gate suite for current phase. Generate report:
- Per-gate: pass/fail status, duration, output summary
- Aggregate: total gates, passed, failed, skipped
- Phase readiness: boolean (all gates pass → ready to advance)
- Detailed failure log for each failed gate with stderr/stdout capture
Log format: JSON for machine parsing + human-readable summary.

Scope:

Validation plan:

- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- targeted cargo test for fsqlite-planner + fsqlite-wal","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:16:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oan","title":"Spec evolution viz: lazy-load doc render dependencies (hljs/markdown-it/dompurify/diff2html)","description":"## Goal

Speed up the specs-evolution visualization page by **lazy-loading heavy render dependencies** (highlight.js, markdown-it, DOMPurify, Diff2Html) only when they are actually needed, without breaking offline viewing or security guarantees.

Primary artifact: `visualization_of_the_evolution_of_the_frankensqlite_specs_document_from_inception.html`.

## Motivation

Today the viz page pulls multiple large JS/CSS libraries up-front. That increases load time, makes the page feel sluggish on weaker machines, and is unnecessary because many users only need the timeline view (not syntax highlighting or diff rendering) for most of their session.

## Non-Negotiables

- **Security:** All rendered markdown/diff HTML MUST be sanitized (DOMPurify or equivalent). No unsafe innerHTML from untrusted text without sanitization.
- **Correctness:** Rendering output must be identical to the current behavior once the relevant dependency is loaded.
- **No feature loss:** Code highlighting, markdown rendering, and side-by-side diffs must still work.
- **Deterministic diagnostics:** When lazy loading fails, the UI must degrade gracefully (plain text) and emit clear logs.

## Implementation Plan

1. **Dependency loader**
   - Add a small loader that can dynamically inject `<script>` / `<link>` tags (or `import()` when feasible).
   - Provide explicit, idempotent `ensure_*()` entrypoints:
     - `ensure_markdown()` (markdown-it + DOMPurify)
     - `ensure_diff()` (Diff2Html + CSS)
     - `ensure_highlight()` (hljs + CSS)
   - Enforce one-time initialization and cache loaded state.

2. **Trigger points (lazy boundaries)**
   - Load markdown renderer only when a markdown panel is opened/visible.
   - Load Diff2Html only when a diff panel is opened/visible.
   - Load highlight.js only when code blocks exist in the currently-rendered DOM.
   - Optional: use `IntersectionObserver` to prefetch dependencies when a panel is near-viewport.

3. **Graceful fallback**
   - If markdown libs fail to load: show raw markdown (escaped) and log an error banner.
   - If Diff2Html fails: show unified diff text (escaped) instead of side-by-side HTML.
   - If hljs fails: keep `<pre><code>` unhighlighted.

4. **Observability**
   - Add structured console logs (single-line JSON) for:
     - dependency load start/finish/failure (include URL, ms, attempt count)
     - render pass start/finish (include panel type, node counts)
     - fallback activation reasons
   - Track simple perf markers (e.g. `performance.mark`) so we can measure TTI and panel-open latency.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Initial page load does not request markdown-it/DOMPurify/Diff2Html/hljs unless the corresponding panel is opened.
- Opening a markdown panel renders correctly within a reasonable latency budget (target < 300ms on warm cache).
- Opening a diff panel renders side-by-side diff correctly (same output as before).
- No console errors on default navigation flows.
- Fallback behavior is correct and clearly logged when any dependency load fails.

## Test Plan

### Unit-ish Tests (lightweight)

- Loader idempotency: calling `ensure_*()` twice does not inject duplicate tags.
- Failure path: simulate failed script load (bad URL) and verify fallback path is taken + logs emitted.

### E2E Smoke Script

Create a script that:
1. Serves the HTML via `python3 -m http.server`.
2. Launches a headless browser (system chromium) to load the page.
3. Opens a markdown panel and a diff panel.
4. Asserts the DOM contains expected sentinel strings for rendered markdown and rendered diff.
5. Captures console logs to a file for debugging (dependency load timings + fallback reasons).

Note: keep this script self-contained and runnable without adding a Node toolchain to the repo.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:02:26.676966588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T03:15:20.269743887Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T03:15:20.269713169Z","close_reason":"Implemented lazy dependency loading for markdown-it/DOMPurify/Diff2Html/highlight.js in visualization HTML with idempotent loaders, structured JSON dependency/render/fallback logs, and graceful plaintext fallbacks. Added and passed e2e/spec_viz_smoke.sh (test_e2e_bd_3oan).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oan","depends_on_id":"bd-1wx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:52:32.008657821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oan","depends_on_id":"bd-2w76","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T10:19:27.025046696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":319,"issue_id":"bd-3oan","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests Required (Explicit `test_*` names)

1. **test_dependency_loader_idempotent_markdown**: `ensure_markdown()` called twice injects scripts/styles exactly once.
2. **test_dependency_loader_idempotent_diff**: `ensure_diff()` idempotency.
3. **test_dependency_loader_idempotent_highlight**: `ensure_highlight()` idempotency.
4. **test_dependency_loader_failure_falls_back_plaintext**: Simulate failed load; verify raw escaped markdown/diff fallback.
5. **test_render_output_stable_after_lazy_load**: Rendering output matches pre-lazy-load behavior for a fixed fixture.

## E2E Smoke Script (Concrete)

- **e2e/spec_viz_smoke.sh** (planned):
  - Serve the HTML via `python3 -m http.server`.
  - Launch a headless browser to open the page.
  - Open a markdown panel and a diff panel.
  - Assert sentinel strings exist in the DOM.
  - Capture console logs to an artifact file.

## Logging Requirements

- Emit one-line JSON console logs for:
  - dependency load start/finish/failure: `dep`, `url`, `duration_ms`, `attempt`
  - render pass start/finish: `panel`, `node_count`, `duration_ms`
  - fallback activation: `panel`, `reason`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Default navigation triggers zero network requests for markdown/diff/highlight deps until the relevant panel is opened.
- When deps load successfully, rendered output matches historical behavior.
- When deps fail, UI degrades gracefully and logs explain what happened.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:30:42Z"},{"id":637,"issue_id":"bd-3oan","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

- [ ] Rendering libraries loaded when user first interacts with feature requiring them
- [ ] Graceful fallback if CDN unavailable: show raw content or error message
- [ ] No render-blocking scripts in critical path

","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:23:24Z"},{"id":1140,"issue_id":"bd-3ppz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Task: TUI framework setup with frankentui and channel-based data feed

### What
Set up the TUI binary and core infrastructure using frankentui (at /dp/frankentui), with a channel-based architecture that allows background test threads to feed data to the UI.

### Architecture
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Background Threads                               │
│  ┌─────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  │
│  │Benchmark │  │Corruption│  │  Correctness   │  │
│  │ Runner   │  │ Recovery │  │  Verification  │  │
│  └────┬─────┘  └────┬─────┘  └──────┬─────────┘  │
│       │              │               │            │
│       └──────────────┼───────────────┘            │
│                      │ mpsc channels               │
│                      ▼                            │
│            ┌─────────────────┐                    │
│            │  Event Loop     │                    │
│            │  (frankentui)   │                    │
│            └───────┬─────────┘                    │
│                    │                              │
│  ┌─────────────────┼─────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  Panel 1   │  Panel 2   │  Panel 3  │ P4  │   │
│  │ Benchmark  │ Recovery   │ Correct.  │ Sum │   │
│  └────────────┴────────────┴───────────┴─────┘   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Channel Messages
```rust
pub enum DashboardEvent {
    BenchmarkProgress { name: String, ops_per_sec: f64, elapsed: Duration },
    BenchmarkComplete { name: String, results: BenchmarkResults },
    CorruptionInjected { page: u32, pattern: String },
    RecoveryAttempt { group: u32, symbols_available: u32, needed: u32 },
    RecoverySuccess { page: u32, decode_proof: String },
    RecoveryFailure { page: u32, reason: String },
    CorrectnessCheck { workload: String, frank_hash: String, csqlite_hash: String, match_: bool },
    StatusMessage(String),
}
```

### frankentui Integration
- Review frankentui's widget system and layout primitives
- Use its app framework for event loop + terminal management
- Keyboard: Tab to switch panels, q to quit, r to run/restart
- Mouse support if frankentui provides it

### Headless Mode
When `--headless` flag is passed:
- Skip TUI initialization
- Still run all tests/benchmarks
- Output JSON results to stdout or file
- For CI environments without terminal

### Acceptance Criteria
- TUI binary compiles and launches
- Shows 4 panel layout (even if panels are placeholder text)
- Channel-based data feed works (send test events, verify display updates)
- Keyboard navigation between panels
- Headless mode outputs JSON
- Terminal resize handled gracefully
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:24:47Z"},{"id":1169,"issue_id":"bd-3ppz","author":"WildFalcon","text":"Progress update (WildFalcon):\n- Fixed headless JSON output for e2e-dashboard: changed DashboardEvent::StatusMessage from newtype to struct variant so #[serde(tag="type")] serializes. profile-db — Generate JSON metadata for golden database files

USAGE:
    profile-db [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
    --golden-dir <DIR>    Directory containing golden .db files
                          (default: sample_sqlite_db_files/golden)
    --output-dir <DIR>    Directory for JSON output files
                          (default: sample_sqlite_db_files/metadata)
    --db <NAME>           Profile only this database file (e.g. beads_viewer.db)
    --pretty              Pretty-print JSON output
    -h, --help            Show this help message

EXAMPLES:
    profile-db
    profile-db --pretty
    profile-db --db frankensqlite.db --pretty
    profile-db --golden-dir /tmp/dbs --output-dir /tmp/meta

realdb-e2e — Differential testing of FrankenSQLite vs C SQLite

USAGE:
    realdb-e2e <SUBCOMMAND> [OPTIONS]

SUBCOMMANDS:
    corpus scan             Discover SQLite databases under /dp
    corpus import           Copy selected DBs into golden/ with checksums
    corpus verify           Verify golden copies against checksums.sha256
    run                     Execute an OpLog workload against an engine
    bench                   Run the benchmark matrix (Criterion)
    corrupt                 Inject corruption into a working copy

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help              Show this help message

EXAMPLES:
    realdb-e2e corpus scan
    realdb-e2e corpus scan --root /dp --max-depth 4
    realdb-e2e corpus import --db beads.db --tag beads
    realdb-e2e corpus verify
    realdb-e2e run --engine sqlite3 --db beads-proj-a --workload commutative_inserts --concurrency 4
    realdb-e2e run --engine fsqlite --db beads-proj-a --workload hot_page_contention --concurrency 8
    realdb-e2e bench --db beads-proj-a --preset all
    realdb-e2e corrupt --db beads-proj-a --strategy page --page 1 --seed 42

realdb-e2e corpus — Manage the SQLite database fixture corpus

USAGE:
    realdb-e2e corpus <ACTION> [OPTIONS]

ACTIONS:
    scan        Discover SQLite databases under configured roots
    import      Copy a discovered database into golden/ with checksums
    verify      Verify all golden copies match their checksums entries

SCAN OPTIONS:
    --root <DIR>        Root directory to scan (default: /dp)
    --max-depth <N>     Maximum traversal depth (default: 6)
    --header-only       Only show files with valid SQLite magic header

IMPORT OPTIONS:
    --db <NAME>         Source database path or discovery name
    --tag <LABEL>       Classification tag (beads, sample, test, etc.)

VERIFY OPTIONS:
    --checksums <PATH>  Path to checksums file (default: sample_sqlite_db_files/checksums.sha256)
